title: Astilbe pinnata
intro: Astilbe pinnata, also known as Chinese astilbe, is a plant species native to China, characterized by its feathery foliage and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Astilbe pinnata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Astilbe pinnata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, coughs, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
taxonomy: Astilbe pinnata is a member of the family Saxifragaceae. This plant is native to East Asia and is also known as the Chinese astilbe. It belongs to the genus Astilbe, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as false goatsbeards. Astilbe pinnata is an herbaceous perennial that typically grows in moist woodland environments. It is a relatively rare species, but is highly prized by gardeners for its ornamental value.
